signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.03 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage sustains the bid despite Iran-related energy market disruptions. US Treasury Secretary Bessent confirms naval efforts to open the Strait of Hormuz amid an 8-10 million barrel oil deficit. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (long% 30.6%) remains crowded; while VIX at 17.2 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant, geopolitical escalation toward 160.00 triggers BoJ intervention risk. JPY safe-haven demand is currently secondary to the massive yield deficit.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7193 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 87% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence indicates retail is aggressively fading the uptrend. Institutional positioning is approaching the 80% extreme threshold, suggesting late-trend exhaustion. RBA at 4.10% vs Fed 3.75% provides a 35bp carry premium, but retail capitulation risk is elevated if price extends higher. Watch for a break of 0.7200 to trigger a retail short-squeeze.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. Speculators are heavily positioned for NZD weakness despite the central bank's hawkish stance. BNY reports RBNZ pushing back on supply-shock hikes, but the OIS bias remains tilted toward tightening. If the RBNZ maintains its hike bias at the next meeting, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk. NZD/USD at 0.5894.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.725 vs 252d norm of -0.142 (z=+1.89σ). The pair is decoupling from traditional equity drivers, moving in lockstep with European indices rather than acting as a funding currency. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (175bp spread) continues to favor USD carry, keeping EUR/USD upside structurally limited at 1.1718. The correlation break suggests EUR is currently being traded as a pro-cyclical risk asset rather than a macro-divergence play.
